mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strong construction activity and household investment, which drives upstream industrial demand and employment, leading to higher GDP growth and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST vs 60-day forward returns below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window, or directional accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated consumer price inflation erodes real cash flows and raises discount rates via tighter monetary policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between CPIAUCSL level and next 20-day returns is positive (>0.1) over any 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5%, it indicates a weakening labor market that will reduce aggregate demand and corporate profits, causing negative equity returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns, or the average return after trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decisive decline in the federal funds rate by more than 50 basis points over a three-month period signals aggressive monetary easing that lowers the discount rate and boosts equity prices, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day return following trigger events is not positive and statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low (≤3%) and industrial production is expanding (index > 100), the economy experiences accommodative monetary policy and robust manufacturing output, creating a strong tailwind for corporate earnings and stock prices.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal with 20-day returns below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or directional accuracy below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains (PAYEMS monthly change > 200K) combined with robust retail sales (RSAFS > 700B) indicate a virtuous cycle of employment and consumption that directly boosts corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive equity returns.
failure_condition: If the mean forward return after co-trigger is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or if less than 50% of co-triggered windows yield positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPI YoY > 3%), the federal funds rate level becomes a stronger negative predictor of equity returns because further increases tighten financial conditions aggressively, while cuts may be insufficient to boost growth. The signal is amplified in this regime.
failure_condition: The inverse correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is weaker in the high inflation regime than in the low inflation regime, or the average return after FEDFUNDS increase is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales figures reflect immediate consumer demand, which directly feeds into corporate earnings expectations and drives short-term momentum in equity markets, producing positive returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of RSAFS with 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or if the mean return in top quintile of RSAFS is not positive.
